key_rights: To be protected from products and services that are dangerous to your life and property (Safety).
To know about the quality, quantity, purity, standard, and price of what you buy (Information).
To have access to a variety of goods and services at fair prices (Choice).
To have your concerns considered in appropriate forums (Be Heard).
To seek solutions against unfair business practices or exploitation (Redressal).
To be educated about your consumer rights (Awareness).
important_provisions: Establishment of the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) to investigate violations, recall unsafe goods, stop misleading advertisements, and impose penalties.
Creation of Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions (District, State, and National) for filing complaints and ordering businesses to fix issues, replace products, or provide compensation.
Promotion of mediation as a way to settle disputes amicably.
Product Liability holding manufacturers, service providers, and sellers responsible for harm caused by defective products, applicable to online and offline transactions.
important_penalties: Penalties for not following directions from the CCPA.
Penalties for publishing false or misleading advertisements.
Penalties for manufacturing, selling, or distributing adulterated or fake (spurious) goods.
Penalties for not complying with orders from the Consumer Commissions.
who_can_benefit: Ordinary citizens when they buy goods or use services (consumers).
